title: Monetary Policy Report—January 2026
summary: The Bank of Canada held its overnight rate target at 2.25% in January 2026, with inflation remaining near the 2% target and growth expected to average around 1.25% over the projection horizon. US tariffs continue to disrupt the Canadian economy, and the future of the Canada-US-Mexico trade agreement remains highly uncertain, creating elevated risks to the outlook.
key_points: Bank of Canada maintained target overnight rate at 2.25%; Bank Rate at 2.5% and deposit rate at 2.20%
CPI inflation remains close to the 2% target over the projection horizon
Canadian economic growth expected to average around 1.25% over the projection horizon
US tariffs and trade uncertainty continue to weigh on Canadian economic growth
Canada-US-Mexico Agreement is under review with unclear future outcomes
Canadian businesses are expanding exports to countries other than the United States and reducing reliance on US imports
Geopolitical uncertainty and trade risks are described as unusually high
Global growth supported by AI investment boom and fiscal stimulus in major economies
why_it_matters: This quarterly monetary policy report provides the Bank of Canada's official assessment of economic conditions and forward guidance for Canada. The maintained 2.25% rate reflects a balanced outlook with inflation near target, but elevated trade uncertainty from US tariffs and the CUSMA review creates significant downside risks for Canadian growth.
macro_context: Canada faces a challenging trade environment with elevated US tariffs disrupting economic activity. The Bank projects excess supply will roughly offset upward cost pressures, keeping inflation near 2% despite trade disruptions. The CUSMA review introduces significant structural uncertainty as outcomes could have wide-ranging impacts on the Canadian economy.

issues_found: verification_confidence is incorrectly set to 0 in the record; llm_review.initial_confidence is 7, suggesting the correct value should be 7
The 90-day threshold calculation in human_review.notes contains a minor factual error: 75 days is correctly within the 90-day window, not 'approximately 75 days before TODAY'
source.url is empty in source metadata, which is a data quality issue for archival purposes

notes: Source is a high-tier domain (Bank of Canada) and directly relevant to macro catalysts through official monetary policy assessment. Contains Bank of Canada's official economic projections and rate decision. The Governing Council members are named (Tiff Macklem, Carolyn Rogers, Toni Gravelle, Sharon Kozicki, Nicolas Vincent, Rhys Mendes, Michelle Alexopoulos). Rate decision details are clearly stated: held at 2.25% overnight target. Trade policy uncertainty from US tariffs and CUSMA review is the dominant macro catalyst discussed.
